# flashduty_schedule (Resource)

Manages a Flashduty on-call schedule.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Simple daily rotation schedule
resource "flashduty_schedule" "primary_oncall" {
  schedule_name = "Primary On-Call"
  description   = "Primary on-call rotation for the SRE team"

  layers = [
    {
      layer_name     = "Primary Layer"
      mode           = 0
      layer_start    = 1704038400
      rotation_unit  = "day"
      rotation_value = 1
      groups = [
        {
          group_name = "SRE Group"
          members = [
            {
              role_id    = 0
              person_ids = [75340551232454]
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Schedule with advanced settings
resource "flashduty_schedule" "advanced" {
  schedule_name = "Advanced Schedule"
  description   = "Schedule with restrictions, day mask, and notifications"

  layers = [
    {
      layer_name     = "Business Hours"
      mode           = 0
      layer_start    = 1704038400
      rotation_unit  = "week"
      rotation_value = 1
      fair_rotation  = true
      restrict_mode  = 1
      restrict_periods = [
        {
          restrict_start = 32400
          restrict_end   = 64800
        }
      ]
      day_mask = {
        repeat = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
      }
      groups = [
        {
          group_name = "Weekday Team"
          members = [
            {
              role_id    = 0
              person_ids = [75340551232454]
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]

  notify = {
    advance_in_time = 3600
    by = {
      follow_preference = true
      personal_channels = ["email"]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) The description of the schedule.
- `layers` (Attributes List) The schedule layers (rotation rules). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers))
- `schedule_name` (String) The name of the schedule.

### Optional

- `notify` (Attributes) Notification settings for the schedule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--notify))
- `status` (Number) The status of the schedule. 0 = enabled.
- `team_id` (Number) The ID of the team that owns this schedule.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the schedule.

<a id="nestedatt--layers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers`

Required:

- `groups` (Attributes List) The on-call groups for this layer. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--groups))
- `layer_name` (String) The name of the layer.
- `layer_start` (Number) The start time of the layer (Unix timestamp).
- `mode` (Number) The mode of the layer. 0 = normal, 1 = temporary.
- `rotation_unit` (String) The rotation unit. Valid values: `hour`, `day`, `week`, `month`.
- `rotation_value` (Number) The rotation value.

Optional:

- `day_mask` (Attributes) Day of week mask. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--day_mask))
- `fair_rotation` (Boolean) Whether to enable fair rotation.
- `handoff_time` (Number) The handoff time in seconds.
- `layer_end` (Number) The end time of the layer (Unix timestamp).
- `mask_continuous_enabled` (Boolean) Whether continuous mask is enabled.
- `restrict_mode` (Number) The restriction mode. 0 = none.
- `restrict_periods` (Attributes List) Time restriction periods. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--restrict_periods))

<a id="nestedatt--layers--groups"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.groups`

Required:

- `members` (Attributes List) The members of the group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--groups--members))

Optional:

- `group_name` (String) The name of the group.

<a id="nestedatt--layers--groups--members"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.groups.members`

Required:

- `person_ids` (List of Number) The list of person IDs.

Optional:

- `role_id` (Number) The role ID.



<a id="nestedatt--layers--day_mask"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.day_mask`

Required:

- `repeat` (List of Number) Days of week (0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday).


<a id="nestedatt--layers--restrict_periods"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.restrict_periods`

Required:

- `restrict_end` (Number) Restriction end time (seconds from midnight).
- `restrict_start` (Number) Restriction start time (seconds from midnight).



<a id="nestedatt--notify"></a>
### Nested Schema for `notify`

Optional:

- `advance_in_time` (Number) Advance notification time in seconds.
- `by` (Attributes) Notification channel settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--notify--by))
- `fixed_time` (Attributes) Fixed time notification settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--notify--fixed_time))
- `webhooks` (Attributes List) Webhook notification settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--notify--webhooks))

<a id="nestedatt--notify--by"></a>
### Nested Schema for `notify.by`

Optional:

- `follow_preference` (Boolean) Whether to follow user preference.
- `personal_channels` (List of String) Personal notification channels (e.g., `email`, `sms`, `phone`).


<a id="nestedatt--notify--fixed_time"></a>
### Nested Schema for `notify.fixed_time`

Required:

- `cycle` (String) Notification cycle (e.g., `day`).
- `start` (String) Start time string.


<a id="nestedatt--notify--webhooks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `notify.webhooks`

Required:

- `settings` (String) Webhook settings as JSON string. Use `jsonencode()` to construct.
- `type` (String) Webhook type (e.g., `feishu`, `dingtalk`, `slack`).
